floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: sp[8] oversize is LOAD-BEARING for the 0x48 frame (saves at 0x30 need array end 0x30); strong clean lead: the 6 slots factor as TWO 4-word entries -> struct entries[2] (2x16B) gives
The 'search space is closed' proof is FALSE: GCC 2.7.2 reserves frame bytes for ordinary LIVE locals with zero stores (phantom slot), witnessed by tslLineG5Init - a COMPLETED-C, cheat-free, sandbox-0

frontier.hypothesis: A pure-C form exists for func_80037540 with get_frame_size in [25,32] arising from LIVE locals (a phantom slot), emitting only the target's six stores - no unwritten tail, no dead declaration, no owner carve-out needed.
frontier.mechanism: cc1 allocates stack temps for computations it later register-allocates away (proven: HImode bitwise temps). The hunt is for which live-code construct induces a 1-8 byte temp in THIS function's shape. Do NOT re-try index locals (s16/u16/u8/long long - all measured vars=24).
frontier.next_probe: Sweep on the vars= instrument (bash tmp/grind/func_80037540/s2/build.sh; extend sweep2.py). Characterize WHICH GCC pass allocates the temp: run cc1 -da on the tslLineG5Init reproducer and find the assign_stack_local/assign_stack_temp call site (expand vs reload), then enumerate every construct that reaches it. Candidates not yet measured: HImode/QImode bitwise ops on the count/index args; a struct-valued temp; a SAVE_EXPR-inducing expression; the (s32)&SpecialCam base as a typed 8-byte-record pointer with sub-word arithmetic.

frontier.hypothesis: The 28 census functions with untouched reserved locals are a training set: several are already matched, so their C shows exactly which live-code constructs produce phantom slots of each size (4, 8, 12, 16, 32 bytes) under this compiler.
frontier.mechanism: Empirical catalog of phantom-slot inducers, harvested from functions whose matching C is already known - the fastest route to the specific inducer func_80037540 needs (it needs +1..+8 bytes on top of s32 sp[6]).
frontier.next_probe: For each of the 28 (tmp/grind/func_80037540/s2/bb2_frame_slack2.txt), if it has a C body in src/, compile it and read vars=; tabulate construct -> phantom bytes. Start with the ones needing a small slot: cpu_get_dist, func_800165F8, func_80023648, func_8003032C, func_80030900, func_800400F8 (all vars=8).

frontier.hypothesis: func_8003DBE4's committed 's32 buf[2]' dead array (distance 21, ACTIVE in queue) is unnecessary - the target's 8 extra frame bytes are a phantom slot from its own live locals, exactly as in its caller tslLineG5Init.
frontier.mechanism: Same phantom-slot mechanism; func_8003DBE4 is in the same file and same call chain, and its dead array was added specifically to 'reserve the target's 8 extra frame bytes'.
frontier.next_probe: Not this function - hand to the driver. Delete the dead array, compile, read vars=; if it still reaches the target frame, an open cheat retires for free.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): s32 sp[8] oversized by 2 elements: sp[6] and sp[7] are never written or read; callee is invoked with count=6, so only sp[0]-sp[5] have semantic purpose. The 2 unused elements inflate the stack frame from 0x40 (sp[6] would give 16+24+24=64) to 0x48 (sp[8] gives 16+32+24=72), matching the target prologue `addiu $sp,$sp,-0x48`. This is the 'oversized local array frame-coercion' pattern (inline-asm-policy expanded catalog: 'Unused fixed-size local arrays — s32 buf[N]; declared with no use, to force GCC to reserve frame bytes'). The correct fix is to find a C form where a 0x48 frame arises naturally (e.g., a genuine 8-element array if the original spec used one, a second local variable, or a different struct layout) — or to determine whether sp[6]/sp[7] ARE legitimately read by marionation_camera_Init_80037468 despite the count=6 argument. Until resolved, this function is cheat-carried and must remain INCOMPLETE.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)

Do NOT commit any form whose locals bound is chosen to fit the 0x48 frame -- the oracle attests no bound (sp[7] and sp[8] are byte-indistinguishable), so any such number is a guess and the unwritten-tail array stays forbidden; canonical-asm is also refused (gate verdict C, zero hand-coded signals). The match must land with a locals object whose SIZE is justified by evidence external to the frame: run the unprobed frontier item -- recover the bios_Exec argv block's real arity from the exec'd program (MOVOVL.EXE / NDATA overlays) or other Exec launch sites.

Do NOT land, commit, or mark done ANY form for func_80037540 whose locals bound is chosen to produce the 0x48 frame (sp[7], sp[8], struct relabelings, or a second dead object) — the unwritten-tail family is off the sanctioned list and the bound is attested by nothing; the ONLY sanctioned next step is to assemble a SOTN-master-branch (+oot/papermario/MGS/VS/ESA) evidence census on the capacity-declared-partially-filled-buffer family and put it to the owner for a carve-out ruling.

Do NOT land, commit, or mark done any unwritten-tail form (sp[7], sp[8], struct relabelings, or any second dead object) -- the family is not on the frozen SOTN-accepted list and only the OWNER can add it; do NOT re-grind the search space (it is closed by proof: the frame equation pins locals to [25,32] and the target writes 24, so every branch is measured) and do NOT re-derive or re-litigate this a-fortiori argument to a Judge; the item is HELD, INCOMPLETE, blocked on a single owner yes/no on the capacity-declared-partially-filled-buffer family, with the evidence packet now complete -- if the owner GRANTS it, land sp[8] with a /* FAKE */ annotation documenting the get_frame_size [25,32] range per the SOTN `_pad[40] // needs to be 33-40 bytes` convention, plus layer-1+layer-2 review.
